rationale: The citing paper focuses on yes/no vs. opt-in email response formats in workplace wellness programs, while the cited paper analyzes sponsored-search ad position effects on CTR, conversion, and profit in ad auctions; the notes indicate little direct topical or methodological overlap. Given the only matched evidence is a single DOI hit with no in-text context, this looks most like an incidental/background citation (e.g., click vs. conversion distinction) rather than substantive reliance.
